extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won stage 4 sprint after Lotto-Soudal successfully chased back following separation on Ardennes climbs with 8km remaining.
tactical_patterns: Lotto-Soudal demonstrated superior chase capacity when Ewan dropped, using Sweeney/van der Zande to regain contact within final 8km.
Ardennes climbs failed to create GC selection despite expectations, revealing course was inadequate for lightweight climbers' advantage.
Breakaway management allowed six-rider group with riders 5+ minutes down, indicating peloton confidence in controlling modest climbing circuit.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: Won by multiple bike lengths after being dropped late, suggesting superior form recovery and sprint speed over stage race fatigue.
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: Dropped from lead group with 8km remaining on Ardennes climbs, exposing weakness on rolling terrain compared to pure flat stages.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Positioned at front despite GC already secured, suggesting team using stage for sprint leadout practice rather than GC defense.
rider_signals.DE BUYST Jasper: positive: Delivered final leadout for Ewan while finishing 5th himself, demonstrating dual sprint/leadout capability unusual for domestiques.
